key_points: Federalism is a form of power sharing between different levels of government
The Constitution clearly divides powers between Union and State governments
Subjects in governance are divided into Union List, State List, and Concurrent List
The judiciary acts as the guardian of the Constitution
Regional parties play an important role in strengthening federalism
examples: Union List includes subjects like defence and foreign affairs
State List includes police and agriculture
Concurrent List includes subjects such as education
Regional parties influence national policies in coalition governments
exam_tips: Understand the differences between the three constitutional lists
Learn examples of subjects in each list
Remember how regional parties strengthened federalism after 1990
Be able to explain the role of the judiciary in federal systems
